title: zerna condensata subsp. australis
intro: Zerna condensata subsp. australis, also known as Australian milk vetch, is a plant species in the legume family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Australian milk vetch is often used as a powder or tea, and can also be made into a salve or cream to be applied topically.
